_about: WS-1 intake eval. Each item is a 4-6 sentence free-text dictation plus the ground-truth structured fields a correct CaseContext should capture. ids mirror eval/cases.json. Scored by eval/intake_eval.py and eval/quality_gate.py: side/level via the offline deterministic parser (real); goal/comorbidities + missing_critical==0 via the full parse path fed the ground-truth JSON (validates capture, not model quality — live model grade deferred to a keyed run).

dictations.dictation: This is a 62 year old woman with twelve months of progressive gait imbalance and right C6 radiculopathy with hand clumsiness. MRI shows C5-6 spondylotic cord compression with myelomalacia. Her history is notable for hypertension and type 2 diabetes, and she takes aspirin daily. We are planning an anterior cervical discectomy and fusion at C5-6 to decompress the cord and the right C6 root.

This is a 58 year old man with a year of mechanical low back pain and right L5 radiculopathy that worsens with walking. Imaging shows a grade I degenerative spondylolisthesis at L4-5 with foraminal stenosis on the right. He has hypertension and a remote smoking history. We plan an L4-5 transforaminal lumbar interbody fusion with pedicle screw instrumentation to decompress the right L5 root and stabilize the segment.

A 41 year old man presents with six weeks of severe left leg pain in an S1 distribution with a positive straight leg raise, failing conservative care. MRI shows a left paracentral L5-S1 disc herniation compressing the traversing S1 nerve root. He is otherwise healthy and on no anticoagulation. We plan a left L5-S1 microdiscectomy to decompress the S1 root.

This is a 64 year old woman with progressive lower-extremity weakness, a spastic gait, and a T6 sensory level. MRI demonstrates a left-sided intradural extramedullary mass at T6 consistent with a meningioma, compressing the spinal cord. She has well-controlled hypertension. We plan a T6 laminectomy with intradural resection of the tumor and watertight dural closure.

A 47 year old man presents with left-sided progressive sensorineural hearing loss and tinnitus, with serviceable hearing preserved on audiometry. MRI demonstrates a 2.5 cm left cerebellopontine angle vestibular schwannoma indenting the brainstem. He is otherwise healthy with no anticoagulation. The plan is a left retrosigmoid resection with attempted hearing preservation and continuous facial nerve and ABR monitoring.

This is a 52 year old woman with progressive right facial numbness, gait ataxia, and headaches. MRI shows a right petroclival meningioma abutting the brainstem and encasing the trigeminal nerve, with mild displacement of the basilar artery. She has hypertension. We plan a right retrosigmoid approach for resection with cranial nerve and brainstem monitoring.

A 49 year old man presents with bitemporal hemianopia and headaches. MRI shows a sellar mass with suprasellar extension elevating the optic chiasm, consistent with a nonfunctioning pituitary macroadenoma; endocrine workup shows mild hypopituitarism. He has type 2 diabetes. We plan an endoscopic endonasal transsphenoidal resection to decompress the chiasm.

This is a 38 year old woman with left trigeminal neuralgia and mild left facial weakness. MRI with diffusion shows a left cerebellopontine angle epidermoid cyst insinuating around the cranial nerves and brainstem. She is otherwise healthy. We plan a left retrosigmoid craniotomy for microsurgical resection of the cyst with cranial nerve monitoring.

A 65 year old woman presented with a sudden thunderclap headache and was found to have a Hunt-Hess 2 subarachnoid hemorrhage. CT angiography shows a ruptured left middle cerebral artery bifurcation aneurysm with a 6 mm dome. She has hypertension and is a current smoker. We plan a left pterional craniotomy for microsurgical clip ligation with proximal control and indocyanine green videoangiography.

A 54 year old man presented with a severe headache and a Hunt-Hess 3 subarachnoid hemorrhage. CT angiography reveals a ruptured anterior communicating artery aneurysm projecting superiorly. He has hypertension and hyperlipidemia. We plan a right pterional craniotomy for microsurgical clip ligation with bilateral A1 proximal control.

A 59 year old woman presents with a new left pupil-involving third nerve palsy and headache. CT angiography shows a left posterior communicating artery aneurysm arising from the internal carotid artery; there is no subarachnoid hemorrhage. She has hypertension. We plan a left pterional craniotomy for microsurgical clip ligation with carotid proximal control.

This is a 28 year old man with a seizure and a small right parietal hemorrhage. Angiography shows a right parietal arteriovenous malformation, Spetzler-Martin grade II, with superficial feeders and a single superficial draining vein. He is otherwise healthy. We plan staged preoperative embolization followed by microsurgical resection of the AVM.

A 61 year old woman presented with a thunderclap headache and a Hunt-Hess 2 subarachnoid hemorrhage. CT angiography shows a ruptured anterior communicating artery aneurysm with a favorable neck for coiling. She has hypertension. We plan endovascular coil embolization of the aneurysm via a transfemoral approach.

This is a 57 year old man with an incidental 8 mm basilar apex aneurysm found on imaging for headaches. The aneurysm has a wide neck incorporating both P1 origins. He has hypertension and hyperlipidemia. We plan stent-assisted coil embolization of the basilar apex aneurysm with dual antiplatelet therapy.

A 63 year old woman has a large unruptured right cavernous/paraophthalmic internal carotid artery aneurysm causing headaches and mild visual symptoms. The aneurysm is broad-necked and not ideal for primary coiling. She has hypertension. After dual antiplatelet preparation we plan placement of a flow-diverter stent across the aneurysm neck.

A 72 year old man presents two hours after sudden right-sided weakness and aphasia with an NIHSS of 16. CT angiography shows a left middle cerebral artery M1 occlusion with a small core and a large salvageable penumbra on perfusion imaging. He has atrial fibrillation and hypertension. We plan emergent mechanical thrombectomy of the left M1 occlusion.

This is a 38 year old right-handed woman with new word-finding difficulty and focal seizures. MRI shows a non-enhancing left dominant frontal lesion involving the inferior frontal gyrus, suspicious for a diffuse glioma. She is on levetiracetam and is otherwise well. We plan an awake craniotomy with cortical and subcortical language and motor mapping for maximal safe resection.

This is a 66 year old man with a fifteen-year history of Parkinson's disease, now with motor fluctuations and medication-refractory tremor but a good levodopa response. He is on carbidopa-levodopa and has hypertension. We plan bilateral subthalamic nucleus deep brain stimulation with microelectrode recording and awake intraoperative testing.

A 31 year old right-handed woman has drug-resistant focal epilepsy with left mesial temporal sclerosis on MRI and concordant left temporal seizure onset on video-EEG. Neuropsychological testing and Wada confirm left language dominance with adequate contralateral memory. She takes two antiseizure medications. We plan a left anterior temporal lobectomy with amygdalohippocampectomy.

This is a 56 year old man with headaches and an incidental right frontal extra-axial mass. MRI shows a 4 cm right frontal convexity meningioma with a dural tail and surrounding edema, away from eloquent cortex. He is on no anticoagulation and has well-controlled hypertension. The plan is a right frontal craniotomy for a Simpson grade I resection with circumferential dural devascularization.

A 60 year old man presents with two weeks of headaches and mild word-finding difficulty. MRI shows a right temporal heterogeneously enhancing mass with central necrosis and surrounding edema, consistent with a high-grade glioma. He has hypertension and is on dexamethasone. We plan a right temporal craniotomy for maximal safe resection with 5-ALA fluorescence guidance and neuronavigation.

This is a 67 year old woman with known lung adenocarcinoma presenting with headache, nausea, and right-sided dysmetria. MRI shows a right cerebellar hemispheric metastasis with surrounding edema and early fourth-ventricle effacement. She has hypertension and COPD. We plan a suboccipital craniotomy for resection of the cerebellar metastasis with a perioperative CSF diversion plan.

A 44 year old woman presents with headaches and a mild right visual-field deficit. MRI shows a vascular tumor in the left lateral ventricle trigone with mild ventricular enlargement. She is otherwise healthy. We plan a left transcortical approach for resection of the intraventricular tumor with neuronavigation.

A 7 year old boy presents with morning headaches, vomiting, and truncal ataxia. MRI shows a midline fourth-ventricle mass with obstructive hydrocephalus, most consistent with a medulloblastoma. He is otherwise healthy. We plan a suboccipital craniotomy with a telovelar approach for resection, with a perioperative CSF diversion plan for the hydrocephalus.

A 9 year old girl presents with several weeks of headaches, morning vomiting, and left limb clumsiness. MRI shows a left cerebellar hemispheric cystic mass with an enhancing mural nodule, consistent with a pilocytic astrocytoma, and mild hydrocephalus. She is otherwise healthy. We plan a suboccipital craniotomy for gross-total resection of the tumor.

An 8 year old boy presents with short stature, headaches, and bitemporal visual-field loss. MRI shows a suprasellar cystic and solid mass with calcification elevating the optic chiasm, consistent with a craniopharyngioma; he has central hypothyroidism and growth-hormone deficiency. We plan resection of the suprasellar craniopharyngioma with attention to the hypothalamus and optic apparatus.

A newborn boy is delivered with an open lumbosacral myelomeningocele and lower-extremity weakness, with head ultrasound showing mild ventriculomegaly and a Chiari II malformation. He is otherwise stable in the neonatal unit. We plan early operative closure of the myelomeningocele with neural placode reconstruction and watertight dural closure, with a plan to monitor for hydrocephalus.
